payload.title: M86 Select Bus Service Progress Report
payload.publisher: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation
New York City Department of Design and Construction

payload.description: The M86 Bus Route travels from W 87th Street and West End Avenue on the west to E 92nd Street and York Avenue on the east.
Pre-SBS local service on the 86th Street crosstown corridor
Planned SBS conversion along 79th Street, scheduled for spring 2017

payload.limits: Upper East Side to Upper West Side across Central Park
payload.description: Connects the neighborhoods of the Upper East Side and Upper West Side via 86th Street and the Central Park Transverse Road
Planned SBS corridor along 79th Street in Manhattan
Conversion of the M86 crosstown bus route to Select Bus Service, including queue jump bus lanes, off-board fare payment, real-time passenger information, shelters, and pedestrian safety improvements

payload.status: Construction began June 2016; anticipated completion summer 2017
payload.description: Six neckdowns and four bus bulbs being added for pedestrian safety along the M86 SBS route, performed by NYC DDC

Short sections of bus lanes at key intersections to provide bus priority. Westbound queue jump at E 86th Street at 5th Avenue. Eastbound queue jumps at W 86th Street and Central Park West entering Transverse Road, and exiting park at E 84th Street and 5th Avenue.
payload.location_text: E 86th Street at 5th Avenue (westbound); W 86th Street at Central Park West (eastbound); Transverse Road at 5th Avenue (eastbound)

normalized_location.raw_text: E 86th Street at 5th Avenue (westbound); W 86th Street at Central Park West (eastbound); Transverse Road at 5th Avenue (eastbound)

payload.description: Customers pay before boarding using MetroCard or coin fare machines at each station. All-door boarding. Fare enforcement by NYCT security personnel via random inspection.
payload.location_text: All M86 SBS stations except westbound W 86th Street & Broadway, eastbound E 91 St & 1 Ave, and Central Park Transverse Road

normalized_location.raw_text: All M86 SBS stations except westbound W 86th Street & Broadway, eastbound E 91 St & 1 Ave, and Central Park Transverse Road

payload.treatment_kind: real-time passenger information and wayfinding signage
payload.description: Real-time passenger information and wayfinding totems at SBS stations with static wayfinding maps and LED screens showing estimated arrival times from NYCT's Bus Time GPS system.
payload.location_text: Westbound: York Avenue & E 87th Street, Fifth Avenue, Central Park West, Columbus Avenue. Eastbound: Amsterdam Avenue, Columbus Avenue, Central Park West, Madison Avenue, First Avenue.

normalized_location.raw_text: Westbound: York Avenue & E 87th Street, Fifth Avenue, Central Park West, Columbus Avenue. Eastbound: Amsterdam Avenue, Columbus Avenue, Central Park West, Madison Avenue, First Avenue.

payload.description: Existing shelters updated with SBS branding. New shelters to be added at Lexington and Third Avenues as part of bus bulb construction capital project. One bench added at westbound E 86 St & Fifth Avenue.
Curb extensions at crosswalk areas to reduce pedestrian crossing distance. Six neckdowns included in the M86 SBS project.
Curb extensions at bus stops preventing need for buses to pull in and out of moving lane. Four bus bulbs included, in both directions at Lexington and 3rd Avenues.
payload.location_text: Lexington Avenue and 3rd Avenue, both directions

normalized_location.raw_text: Lexington Avenue and 3rd Avenue, both directions

payload.description: M86 Select Bus Service launched after several weeks of SBS station construction and updated road markings installation
M86 SBS Introduction Presentation to Community Board 7
M86 SBS Introduction Presentation to Community Board 8
M86 SBS Implementation Plan Presentation to Community Board 7
M86 SBS Implementation Plan Presentation to Community Board 8
Second Avenue Subway Q service began operation with a stop at 86 St
Construction of pedestrian safety neckdowns and bus bulbs began

payload.claim_text: Prior to SBS, the M86 was the second-busiest crosstown bus route (second to the M14A/D) in Manhattan with the highest per-mile ridership in the city

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: affects_route.

payload.claim_text: Over three-quarters of surveyed riders stated that the new SBS is an improvement over the previous local service

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: affects_route.

payload.claim_text: Ridership measured in August and September 2015, and March and April 2016, was impacted by fare machines at some major stops being out of service due to construction and power outages

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: affects_route.

payload.claim_text: Dwell time improvement would likely have been greater if not for the temporary movement of high-ridership stops at Lexington and 3rd Avenues to the nearside of their intersections to accommodate ongoing capital construction

payload.description: M86 SBS route operates on the 86th Street crosstown corridor
M86 Select Bus Service project implements SBS on the M86 route
NYC DOT and MTA NYCT jointly operate the Select Bus Service program
Community Board 7 participated in M86 SBS outreach via Transportation Committee
Community Board 8 participated in M86 SBS outreach via Transportation Committee
NYC DDC performs construction of pedestrian safety neckdowns and bus bulbs
M86 SBS project includes queue jump bus lanes
M86 SBS project includes off-board fare payment
M86 SBS project includes real-time passenger information and wayfinding signage
Pedestrian safety capital project includes six neckdowns
Pedestrian safety capital project includes four bus bulbs
M86 SBS project launched on July 13, 2015
Pedestrian safety construction began in June 2016
M86 SBS serves as model for M79 SBS conversion
M79 SBS planned along 79th Street corridor
